title: UK extremes ‘new normal’ | Pakistan rains | Trump cuts - Carbon Brief
gist: The UK Met Office's annual 'state of the UK climate' report finds 2025 was the UK's hottest year on record with days above 30C in London quadrupling since the 1980s; China's low-carbon tech exports rose over a third in H1 2026 (lithium batteries +38%, wind turbines +36%, EVs +68.7%); and a BlueGreen Alliance report finds Trump-era policy rollbacks have caused $83bn in delayed or cancelled US clean-energy investment across 223 projects and 111,765 jobs.

insights.claim: The Met Office's annual 'state of the UK climate' report found 2025 was the UK's hottest year on record, with the last four years all ranking among the top five hottest, and the number of days above 30C in London has quadrupled since the 1980s.
insights.speaker: Carbon Brief
insights.so_what: Confirms a structural, accelerating shift in UK climate baseline conditions, relevant for UK infrastructure adaptation investment (cooling, grid capacity, building codes) and insurance/reinsurance risk repricing.
insights.quote: What we used to think of as extreme, we increasingly consider as normal.
insights.locator: 'Unprecedented' changes in UK climate are normalising extremes, report says

insights.claim: The UK has recorded 25 non-consecutive days of 30C or higher so far in 2026, already exceeding the previous benchmark set in 1976.
insights.speaker: Carbon Brief
insights.so_what: A specific, verifiable data point marking 2026 as a record UK heat year, useful for calibrating near-term UK heat-adaptation and energy-demand forecasting models.
insights.locator: 'Unprecedented' changes in UK climate are normalising extremes, report says

insights.claim: China's low-carbon technology exports rose more than a third from January to June 2026, driven by accelerating global energy-transition demand, with lithium battery shipments up 38%, wind turbine exports up 36%, and EV exports up 68.7% year-on-year.
insights.speaker: Carbon Brief
insights.so_what: Quantifies China's dominant and accelerating position as the global supplier of clean-tech hardware, a key data point for assessing China clean-tech manufacturer revenue growth and Western import/tariff policy pressure.
insights.locator: China's green-tech exports surge on energy transition demand

insights.claim: China's new energy vehicle share of the national vehicle fleet reached 13.2%, up 2.9 percentage points year-on-year, and Hainan province became China's first to ban the sale of internal combustion engine vehicles by 2030.
insights.speaker: Carbon Brief
insights.so_what: Hainan's ICE sales ban is a bellwether policy that could be replicated in other Chinese provinces, an early signal for accelerating China domestic EV penetration and potential regulatory templates elsewhere.

insights.claim: A BlueGreen Alliance analysis found Trump administration policy rollbacks -- including the tax-and-spending package that repealed or curtailed Biden-era incentives -- caused the cancellation or delay of $82.9bn in investment across 223 manufacturing and clean-energy projects, at a cost of 111,765 jobs.
insights.speaker: Carbon Brief
insights.so_what: A concrete, quantified estimate of the economic cost of US clean-energy policy reversal -- a hard number investors can use to size the negative earnings/project-pipeline impact on US clean-tech manufacturers and their supply chains.
insights.locator: US: Trump cuts to clean energy linked to $83bn in delayed or cancelled projects

insights.claim: New York became the first US state to halt new data-centre construction, imposing a one-year moratorium on development.
insights.speaker: Carbon Brief
insights.so_what: A first-mover state-level regulatory constraint on data-centre buildout signals potential grid-capacity and power-cost concerns that could spread to other US states, relevant for data-centre and AI-infrastructure siting decisions.

insights.claim: Heavy rain caused a roof collapse that killed 11 people in northwest Pakistan's Khyber Pakhtunkhwa region, while India's Jammu and Kashmir recorded at least 15 cloudburst incidents in 14 days and Bangladesh saw at least 56 deaths from rain-triggered floods with nearly 11,000 people displaced.
insights.speaker: Carbon Brief
insights.so_what: Documents a simultaneous, region-wide South Asian monsoon flooding crisis affecting multiple countries, relevant for humanitarian, reinsurance and South Asian infrastructure/agriculture risk assessment.
insights.locator: Heavy rain collapses roof and kills 11 people in northwest Pakistan

insights.claim: India's hydropower generation declined 21% in June 2026 compared to the prior year due to the El Niño effect.
insights.speaker: Carbon Brief
insights.so_what: A concrete El Niño-driven generation shortfall that could increase India's reliance on coal/gas generation to fill the gap, relevant for Indian power-sector fuel-mix and emissions forecasting.

insights.claim: Potsdam Institute professor Anders Levermann argues climate scientists have overlooked that the transition through tipping points -- not just the eventual endpoint -- will manifest as a period of increasingly dramatic weather volatility, and that no coherent framework exists in climate physics or economics for analysing climate subsystems during these tipping transitions.
insights.speaker: Anders Levermann
entities: Potsdam Institute for Climate Impact Research
insights.so_what: Proposes a contrarian, higher-risk framework for climate scenario planning -- treating the climate as a currently-unstable, reorganizing system rather than one moving smoothly toward a new equilibrium -- with implications for how catastrophe/tail-risk models should be built for insurance and asset allocation.
insights.quote: Instead of analysing future climate as a sequence of quasi-equilibria – as a system that will settle when the world stops emitting carbon – researchers must treat it as an unstable system that is reorganizing while society tries to phase out carbon-based energy.
insights.locator: 'Climate free fall': why the biggest risk to our economies is yet to be recognised

themes.theme: UK adaptation gap warning for Burnham government

insights.claim: Strategic Climate Risks Initiative director Laurie Laybourn warns incoming PM Andy Burnham that closing the UK's 'adaptation gap' -- the difference between the climate society is built for and the one that now exists -- should be a priority, noting that the probability of a 40C UK heatwave before 2040 was assessed at just 0.02% in 2021, yet it occurred the very next year.
insights.speaker: Carbon Brief
insights.so_what: The dramatic gap between the 2021 probability estimate (0.02%) and the actual 2022 outcome is a powerful illustration of climate-model underestimation risk, relevant for stress-testing UK infrastructure and insurance risk models that may still rely on outdated probability assumptions.
insights.quote: in 2021, the chances of a 40C heatwave happening in the UK before 2040 were assessed at 0.02%. Yet the following year, it happened.
insights.locator: Andy Burnham must act fast on the climate

themes.theme: France nuclear and grid stress from heat

insights.claim: 6.3 gigawatts of French nuclear power capacity was offline due to the heatwave -- equivalent to around 14% of national demand -- as unusually warm river water constrained plant cooling capacity.
insights.speaker: Carbon Brief
insights.so_what: Quantifies a recurring structural vulnerability in France's nuclear fleet to heat/river-temperature constraints, a factor relevant to French/European power-price volatility forecasting during future heatwaves.
